geopolitics_summary: EU sanctions on China-Russia tech links; transatlantic trade tensions; Middle East war sustaining energy crisis backdrop.

themes.headline: EU exempts green investments from fiscal spending rules amid energy crisis
themes.why_it_matters: Regulatory flexibility accelerates EU renewable capex & grid upgrades; reduces capex financing friction → faster coal-out, lower long-term power supply costs. Direct Cal+1 curve impact via faster decarbonisation.

themes.headline: EU to curb peak demand via AI-driven household load-shifting as AI/industry demand soars
themes.why_it_matters: Demand-side flattening via AI scheduling reduces peak-hour power prices & congestion risk. Direct downward pressure on DA spreads & peak-load premia; improves grid stability but compresses margin opportunities.

themes.headline: Poland slams EU ETS; Commission to review system in July 2026
themes.why_it_matters: EUA forward curve at risk if July review signals cap-loosening or free-allowance expansion. Political pressure mounts ahead of formal review — key trigger for EUA vol & re-pricing of carbon-cost embedded in power spreads.

themes.headline: US natural gas pipeline capacity additions: 45 Bcf/d planned 2026–27, mostly Texas origin
themes.why_it_matters: Increased US LNG export capacity → lower Henry Hub & global LNG spot prices → downward TTF arbitrage pressure. Indirect bearish for EU gas/power if LNG supply surplus depresses NW Europe equilibrium pricing.

themes.headline: El Niño to intensify heat and extreme weather over coming months globally
themes.why_it_matters: El Niño → elevated summer cooling demand & potential hydro supply tightness in Nordic/Alpine regions. Upward pressure on summer baseload & spot power; intraday volatility risk from renewable intermittency.

watchlist: EU–Trump trade deal plenary vote: June 16. Tariff escalation risk → transatlantic cost inflation → power input costs.
EU ETS Commission review: July 2026. Political consensus on carbon price target / cap tightening / MSR rules critical for Q3–Q4 curve.
US LNG export ramp-up 2026–27. Monitor Henry Hub & spot LNG prices; TTF arb dynamics.
Middle East war escalation risk. Energy security concerns sustaining green capex exemptions & grid investment.
